postal documentation site — https://postal-js.org
Pub/Sub message bus for JavaScript and TypeScript. Wildcard subscriptions, channel-scoped messaging, zero dependencies.

Source repo: https://github.com/postaljs/postal.js
Version: 3.0.0
License: MIT

---

PACKAGES COVERED BY THIS DOCUMENTATION

postal                         Core library (npm)
postal-transport-messageport   MessagePort bridge transport (npm)
postal-transport-broadcastchannel  BroadcastChannel bridge transport (npm)
postal-transport-serviceworker ServiceWorker bridge transport (npm)
postal-transport-childprocess  child_process/cluster IPC transport (npm)
postal-transport-uds           Unix domain socket transport (npm)

---

SITE STRUCTURE

/                              Landing page and quick-start
/guide/introduction/           What postal is, design philosophy, v3 changes
/guide/getting-started/        Installation and first example
/guide/concepts/               Channels, topics, envelopes, wildcards explained
/guide/subscriptions/          subscribe(), publish(), unsubscribe, wildcard patterns
/guide/wiretaps/               addWiretap() — global bus observers
/examples/overview/            Code examples: basic pub/sub, RPC, transports
/tools/overview/               Available transport packages
/migration/v2-to-v3/           Breaking changes from postal v2 to v3
/api/                          Auto-generated API reference (typedoc)

API reference pages:
  /api/functions/getChannel/
  /api/functions/addWiretap/
  /api/functions/addTransport/
  /api/functions/resetChannels/
  /api/functions/resetTransports/
  /api/functions/resetWiretaps/
  /api/classes/PostalTimeoutError/
  /api/classes/PostalRpcError/
  /api/classes/PostalDisposedError/
  /api/type-aliases/Channel/
  /api/type-aliases/Envelope/
  /api/type-aliases/EnvelopeType/
  /api/type-aliases/Transport/
  /api/type-aliases/TransportFilter/
  /api/type-aliases/TransportOptions/
  /api/type-aliases/RequestOptions/
  /api/type-aliases/SubscriberCallback/
  /api/interfaces/ChannelRegistry/

---

CORE CONCEPTS (SUMMARY)

Channels: Named scopes. getChannel("orders") returns a singleton.
Topics: Dot-delimited strings. "order.placed", "user.profile.updated".
Wildcards: * = one segment, # = zero or more segments.
Envelopes: Every message is wrapped in { id, type, channel, topic, payload, timestamp }.
Wiretaps: Global observers via addWiretap(). See all messages. Errors are swallowed.
RPC: request() + handle() — correlation-based, returns a Promise, supports timeouts.
Transports: Bridge across iframes, workers, tabs. Implement { send, subscribe, dispose? }.

---

TRANSPORT PACKAGES

postal-transport-messageport
  Use for: iframes, dedicated workers
  Requires handshake (SYN/ACK over MessagePort)
  Exports: connectToIframe, connectToParent, connectToWorker, connectToHost,
           createMessagePortTransport, PostalHandshakeTimeoutError, ConnectOptions

postal-transport-broadcastchannel
  Use for: same-origin tabs and windows
  No handshake required
  Exports: createBroadcastChannelTransport(name?)

postal-transport-serviceworker
  Use for: tab ↔ ServiceWorker communication
  Dedicated MessagePort per tab, SYN/ACK handshake
  Client entry: connectToServiceWorker(registration, options?)
  SW entry (postal-transport-serviceworker/sw): listenForClients(options?)

postal-transport-childprocess
  Use for: child_process.fork() and cluster IPC
  SYN/ACK handshake over Node.js IPC channel
  Exports: connectToChild, connectToParent, createIPCTransport
  Cluster entry (postal-transport-childprocess/cluster): connectToClusterWorker, connectToClusterPrimary

postal-transport-uds
  Use for: independent Node.js processes on the same machine (no parent/child required)
  Hub-and-spoke via Unix domain socket + NDJSON framing
  Exports: listenOnSocket(path, options?), connectToSocket(path, options?), createSocketTransport
  Node.js only, requires Node >= 22

---

TYPESCRIPT

Two ways to type channels: explicit type map via getChannel<TMap>(name), or ChannelRegistry augmentation for project-wide inference.
RPC topics use { request: X; response: Y } shape in the channel map.
PayloadFor<TMap, Pattern> resolves wildcard subscription payload unions at compile time.
